w:r.w:t: All individuals active with the 412 Youth Zone had the opportunity to participate in a survey in early 2026. Surveys were administered on paper and online.
total active) responded to the survey, for a response rate of

. Most respondents were age 18 or older. About half (49%) of the respondents who reported their age were between 18 and 20 years old, and 40% were 21 to 23 years old. Only 10% were 16 or 17 years old, down from 16% in 2025.
More females (54%) responded to the survey than males (34%). The table below shows how respondents identified by gender and sexual orientation.
Survey Respondents by Gender and Sexual Orientation
Gay, Lesbian, or Same Gender Loving

The tables below display respondents’ racial identities and genders. Please note: Youth Zone participants’ racial identities are self-reported and reflect the full range of how young people describe themselves.
About 52% of survey respondents identified as Black, 28% as White, and 17% as Multiracial.
Youth by Race and Gender (all Youth are Counted Once)
Youth with Full or Partial Racial Identities (Some Youth Are Counted Multiple Times)

90% of youth reported their coaches were trustworthy, and 90% indicated their coach values their opinions about their life. Ratings across all five coach relationship items are shown in the table below.
Satisfaction Ratings for Youth Coaches Over Time
Values my opinions about my life
Is available to me when I need them
Makes me feel heard and understood
The majority of youth communicate with their coaches weekly or monthly. A small number of youth reported their communication was Not Enough.

Survey respondents were asked to describe whether their current housing is safe and stable, meaning they can stay there for at least 90 days. Youth with unstable housing were then asked where they are currently sleeping.
About 70% of respondents reported safe and stable housing. For the remainder, current sleeping arrangements are shown in the table below.
Housing Status and Current Sleeping Arrangements
Can stay 90 days, but not safe
Regardless of their current living situation, if youth experienced unstable housing in the prior six months, they were asked to identify the reason(s).
Reasons for Unstable Housing in the Past 6 Months, by Age¹
No unstable housing in past 6 months
Family/friends no longer let me stay
¹ Youth could report more than one reason for experiencing unstable housing.
46% of all respondents reported being enrolled in school
46% of all respondents reported being employed (44% of these youth are also enrolled in school)
73% of respondents who are in school and unemployed are looking for a job
82% of respondents who are not in school and unemployed are looking for a job
22% of respondents are both not in school and unemployed; 7 of these 44 youth report not completing high school or a GED
Of the 46% of survey respondents that reported being employed, 48% have been at their job for six months or longer.
Length of Employment for Youth Currently Employed

If survey respondents had trouble finding a job in the prior twelve months, they were asked to share some of the reasons why. The most common challenge identified, reported by 41% of youth, was applying and not getting called.
Reasons Youth Have Trouble Finding Jobs (Reasons Given by 2 or More People)
Note: Youth could select more than one option
If the survey respondent lost or left a job in the past year, they were asked to share the reason(s). Sub-reasons for youth who quit are shown indented below the Quit row.
Reasons Youth Lost or Quit a Job in the Past Year (Reasons Given by 2 or More People)
Low pay or not enough hours
Youth were also asked about whether they have a driver’s license and the type of transportation they rely on for work. The table below displays driver’s license status by age.
Of those with a driver’s license, about half regularly have access to a reliable vehicle.
Drivers’ Access to a Reliable Vehicle by Age
All youth were asked about the primary way they get to work when they are employed. The majority rely on public transportation.
Primary Way Youth Get to Work
Youth ages 18 and older were asked to report on whether they are registered to vote. Overall, 62% of eligible respondents reported being registered to vote.
The most common reason youth provided for not registering to vote is that they believe their vote won’t make a difference.
Reasons Youth Report Not Registering to Vote
My vote won't make a difference
I do not know how to register
I am not old enough to vote

This survey includes questions to better understand participants’ experiences at the Zone. Attendance patterns at the Zone vary by age, with older youth coming more frequently than youth ages 16 to 17.
As in prior years, most youth report coming to the 412 Youth Zone downtown to see their coach and to work toward their goals.
What Are the Main Reasons Youth Come to the Youth Zone?
See my Youth Coach or other Zone Staff
For youth who never visit the Zone, or visit less than monthly, they were asked what would make them want to come more frequently.
What Would Make Someone Who Rarely Visits the Zone Want to Come, by Age

Across the core outcome areas in which Youth Zone staff are helping young people make progress, youth reported that their coaches and the Zone have helped them in a variety of ways. 80% of respondents indicated progress supported by the Zone in at least one area.
90% of respondents agreed or somewhat agreed that their coach or the Zone helped them stay focused on their goals.
My Coach or the Youth Zone has Helped Me To… (by Age)
Figure out how to handle problems

Respondents were asked to rate how often they felt respected and accepted for who they are at the Youth Zone. 89% of youth reported staff treat them with respect often or all the time; 76% said the same about their peers at the Zone.
Youth also rated five statements about the program environment on a 1–5 scale. Results are shown in terms of the percentage selecting 4 or 5 (top-2 box).
Participants were asked questions about their use of banks and other ways that they store, receive, and transfer money. Overall, 66% of respondents reported currently having a bank account.
Had an account in the past
Methods Youth Use to Store, Receive, and Transfer Money, by Age
Ways Respondents Use Their Bank Account(s), by Age
None - I don't use my account

Youth were asked to rate on a scale of 0–10 how likely they would be to recommend the Youth Zone to a friend or family member. The Net Promoter Score (NPS) is 55. 69% of respondents were Promoters (9–10).
Finally, youth had the option to share any other comments or feedback they had about the Zone. 53 youth provided additional comments.

I like the YZ. I mainly attend planned activities, but have recently made more of an effort to come down just for socializing + fun more often. I prefer planned activities, or more knowledge of what will be happening ahead of time. Sometimes the website does not have all activities happening listed, and I only know by seeing them on the white board. I would like if the website listed all the activities. It would make it slightly easier to plan days to come down.
I should utilize more the resources available.
Sorry if my paper is confusing, it's my first time and I felt the need to clarify a lot of things
Ms. Stephanie was very kind, knowledgeable, and welcoming. She showed she cared right away and she knows what she is doing. Helps me feel confident, safe, and willing to return.
Very nice, dedicated people who truly have a passion for people. Thank you.
they help youth to developp in their Education
All staff go out of their way to take interest in the youth participants clearly interest, life, problems, etc. They make us feel very heard especially Mrs. Darneisher Martin.
Why don't they help people really need
I love the Youth Zone its and escape from home and its very helpful
I want mister Javon to know im doing well
they help youth develop their education level and they have a good staff who takes care with respect
Mr. Will C is my favorite youth coach + Staff member along with Ms. Tamika C the one that calls Evce her neice + holds her)
I Just Joined today, So I wasn't sure how to answer some of the questions.
Very Great program, Awesome staff that are attentive & willing to help you meet your goals. Very helpful as well with resources and inviting you into a powerful community.
My journey with you guys has helped be with my adult life. Also has helped me change areas of my life, I was having difficulty with.
My coach is better in terms of goat status than Tom Brady that's how clutch she is
I wish it was available to kids who aren't just in cyf my friends that aren't in cyf want to join
Communication should be a bit better. I feel like when I contact you soon through messenger they respond quicker and more attentively than my own Youth Coach.

I had two coaches. I have nicollette first and I loved her. She's amazing and now I have meg and nicollette said she personally picked Meg because she knew that Meg and I would get along because our our combined interests! I love both of them very much and they both have helped me a lot!
My youth coach is always too busy and doesn't make enough time for me
Give my youth coach a raise! He's amazing!
Appreciated a lot of things I wish I can stay in longer than 24
